[d5] Entering Mara's store — she came around the counter for him, hand extended. First real introduction in Millbrook.
[d5] Set the document roll on Mara's counter unprompted — named himself witness, not authority, and deferred to her judgment on whose hands it belongs in. Asked about intake sleeve mate
[d5] Arrived last — one hand fully clear of his coat, reading the forge as a system. Quiet. Measuring.
[d5] Spoke sleeve-iron, intake geometry, the drainage-cut read — the complete engineering picture placed. His unspoken read of the road approach not yet shared with the full group.
[d5] Assigned lead of the east-channel repair crews — accepted with a hand flat on his knee and no ceremony. First morning's scope already sorting.
[d5] Answered Dara's sequencing question directly — lowest intake first, walk it with her before the crew arrives. Clean scope accepted, first morning locked.
[d5] Felt the charge of the partial deathwork. Still has not named what he saw at the drainage cut.

[d9] Calvar walked the lowest intake with Dara on the first morning, confirmed his suspicion about the drainage cut — a slow iron-oxide bleed from a corroded sleeve junction upstream — and marked it on Dara's chart without ceremony.
knownFacts: Noticed something at a road drainage cut on approach; did not name it aloud
Mara Wells is the right first contact — she already knows something is wrong and is ready to hear it confirmed
Millbrook's intake sleeves are iron — filed as a load-bearing engineering fact
Mara Wells is a competent operational contact — her ledger-keeping confirmed it
The document roll's authority question is deferred to Mara's local knowledge
Formally tasked as repair crew lead on the east-channel valve work — Dara Holt is his logistics anchor
Dara Holt is a competent logistics partner — asks the right sequencing question before the work starts
The drainage cut on approach to Millbrook is bleeding iron-oxide from a corroded sleeve junction — the same failure pattern, earlier stage
skillsObserved: precise disclosure of held knowledge when given the right ha
filing operational intelligence in sequence without editoria
self-positioning of authority — named himself witness, not a
precise diagnostic questioning under a first meeting
accepting a clear scope and moving immediately to planning
precise verbal sequencing of valve repair under first-meetin
calvar.statusNote: In the Fell Pell — repair lead assigned, first morning sequenced with Dara. Unspoken read of road drainage cut still held.

maret-doss.name: Maret Doss
maret-doss.role: Innkeeper, Low Lamp Inn — Edge District
maret-doss.description: A compact woman of sixty-odd years; does not ask your name, your business, or where you're going; reads guests fast and accurately; keeps the ledger and the road-wisdom both, and offers them with equal economy.
history: [d4] Confirmed the Weir mark is settled in the ledger. Offered unprompted road advice: the road to Millbrook runs wetter than it looks for the first quarter-mile after a squall.
[d4] Registered the party's arrival without comment — pointed to the back corner booth and went to pour. Asking nothing.
[d5] Had the trough drained and a mug on the table before the party came downstairs — no words, no ceremony. Retreated to her back room.
knownFacts: Does not deal in happy — satisfied is her metric, and she named it plainly
Knows the road to Millbrook's mud behavior after rain — practical and specific
The trough wants draining before the third hour
skillsObserved: accurate ledger-keeping without ceremony
reading a guest's situation and offering exactly relevant pr
maret-doss.statusNote: Behind her door at the Low Lamp Inn — her work for this party complete.

morning-shepherd.name: Shepherd
morning-shepherd.role: Smallholder shepherd, trade track south of the Edge District
morning-shepherd.description: A thin older man in a worn coat, crook in hand — moves his flock with the flat patient tone of long practice; gave the party a wordless nod and kept moving.
history: [d5] Passed on the track while moving a reluctant flock off the verge — exchanged a nod, nothing more.
morning-shepherd.statusNote: On the side-path off the trade track, continuing his morning with his flock.

bren-thalle.name: Bren Thalle
bren-thalle.role: Road opportunist turned toward honest work — miner from lower Wick
bren-thalle.description: A broad man, thirties, worn hands from mining work — grief-carrying and cornered into bad choices by two dry seasons.
history: [d5] Named himself under Huginn's gaze — Bren Thalle, lower Wick settlement, south-slope, three miles past the ford. Dry two seasons. Named the settlement's situation plainly as explana
[d5] Received the hatchet back — handle first, no catch. Tucked it at his belt and started north toward the ford without being asked twice.
[d5] The Attended End read him: no death in frame, a sister to bury come summer, the plan already stalling. Silas's words have reached him.
knownFacts: From lower Wick settlement, south-slope, three miles past the ford
Miner's settlement, two dry seasons running
This was his first time on the road like this — self-reported, unprompted
Silas returned the hatchet as a tool, not a threat — intent named plainly
Directed to Mara Wells in Millbrook for honest work
Has a bruised rib he's been ignoring
Will bury a sister come summer — not yet, but soon
His plan on this track has already run past its best moment and he knows it
bren-thalle.statusNote: Walking north from the limestone bend toward the ford and Millbrook — hatchet at his belt, Curis beside him.

curis.name: Curis
curis.role: Road opportunist turned toward honest work — miner from lower Wick
curis.description: A lean young man of about twenty-two — hollow-eyed from poor eating, grew up in the lower Wick settlement. First time on the road this way, and he volunteered that fact.
history: [d5] Named himself under Huginn's gaze — Curis, same settlement as Bren. Added unprompted that this was the first time. Huginn's shift on the branch was what finally settled him.
[d5] Gathered his provisions and followed Bren north without a word.
[d5] The Attended End read him: something ending that isn't his life. Holding at reach distance, waiting for a cue from the broad man.
knownFacts: From the lower Wick settlement, grew up there
Claimed this was his first time doing this — offered without being asked
Something is ending in him — not his life, but a version of himself he's been trying to keep alive past its natural term
curis.statusNote: Walking north from the limestone bend toward the ford and Millbrook — provisions under his arm.

siol.name: Siol
siol.description: A tall Elven woman of perhaps forty apparent years, long pale coat, hair braided with a dark cord — the stillness of attention held very quiet, not indifference.
history: [d6] Remained still and unspeaking through the attunement — this is a wait she recognizes.
[d6] Said nothing — she has been on this side of this moment before and knows what it is.
[d6] Watching with the expression of someone who has been on this side of this moment before and did not expect to be here again so soon.
[d6] Did not fill the silence. Turned a fraction at Veth's revision but did not contradict it.
[d6] Read the failed attempt in Silas's posture — turned a fraction, set her jaw, said nothing.
[d6] Watched the ward attempt dissipate and held her position — the expression of someone content to let a lesson land on its own.

[d6] Held the edge without entering — but turned fully, coat open, and brought her sustained regard to bear in a way the grove felt. Contributed from outside the circle. The circle went quieter when she did.

[d6] Named plainly what the grove's receiving the ward meant: the Ent received the intention of protection, not the craft — the gesture, not the mechanism. That is the only thing it can receive. Also confirmed she has seen something like the Palework channel from this side before.

[d6] Walked the party from the Ent circle back to the waygate threshold. Named the March setting as north-by-northwest through the gate sequence, and noted the dead post at Raven's Home — eleven years without a warden. Her part complete; she stopped at the threshold the way she stopped at the Ent's edge.
[d6] Walked the party to the sixth arch and held her position at the edge as they transited — her part complete, no ceremony, sustained regard as farewell.
knownFacts: The Palework-through-grove channel is something she has seen the shape of before, or something close to it
Did not fill the silence after the Palework — has been on this side before and knows what it requires.
Siol's quality of attention has weight the grove registers — she does not need to enter to be present.
The Ent receives intention, not craft — the gesture of protection was what landed, not the ward's mechanics
Siol has seen something like a Palework channel from the outside before, or close enough that the shape was familiar
The Pale March gate setting is north-by-northwest — the gate-keeper will know it
Raven's Home has not had a warden in eleven years — named as fact, not warning
Named plainly what the grove's receiving the ward meant: the Ent received the intention of the gift, not the craft — the gesture, not the mechanism.
skillsObserved: plain directional knowledge of the waygate network
reading two strangers' practical needs and sequencing a morn
precise practical briefing on an institution's rules without
plain comparative assessment of two fighters' methods withou
accurate qualification of a living entity's nature without o
sustained regard of a quality that affects a living entity's
siol.statusNote: Remained at the Hub waygate threshold as the party transited — her involvement complete.

orvil.name: Orvil
orvil.description: A compact man with a short baton and the alertness that outlasts boredom — does the gate work so many times that ceremony has been worn away and left something more useful.
history: [d6] Logged the party's crafts at the gate without ceremony — noted Memory and required it peace-bound. Not unfriendly; just running the floor.
[d6] Gave Silas the shape of a morning bout — three exchanges, yield or the sand calls it, crafts declared to opponent. Named the grey-coat at the far arc as a potential match.
[d6] Crossed the sand and brought Tane Solr's acceptance back without fuss — now standing at the center-line between the two fighters, waiting for declarations.
[d6] Stepped back after declarations — bout is properly formed, he is ready to call the start.
[d6] Called the yield cleanly — did not editorialize. Baton down.
[d6] Waiting at the center-line — new fighters, new declaration, same rotation.
[d6] Baton level at the center-line — watching both fighters, no preference, doing his job.
[d6] Called the bout finished without naming a winner — the sand made it plain enough.
knownFacts: Requires craft declaration at entry
Requires weapons peace-bound unless a bout is entered
Morning rotation confirmed
Morning bouts run three exchanges — yield or the sand calls it
A threshold-walker in a grey coat has been waiting for a bout since before first light, with two seconds
skillsObserved: reading a party's composition and equipment in one sweep
reading a situation and offering a clear path without editor
clean call — no editorializing on a controlled finish
orvil.statusNote: At the center-line — baton down, job done.

tane-solr.name: Tane Solr
tane-solr.description: A lean man in a grey coat, unhurried in his movements — handed off his water flask without looking at it, eyes already on the ring. The quality of someone who has been here before and is comfortable waiting.
history: [d6] Silas arrived at his left flank on the opening call — Tane turned from the waist to compensate, weight redistributed off the ankle. Steady. Assessing.
[d6] Yielded on the word 'Ankle' — one chin-drop to Orvil, clean and without argument. Recalibrating now.
[d6] Named his curiosity directly — three years on that ankle, never named in the first breath. Not embarrassed; wants to understand the craft.
[d6] Accepted a second bout on different terms — no deathsense — wants to see what Silas carries without the read. Seconds are preparing him.
[d6] Surprised by the substitution, but shifted into pleased attention when Pell declared iron — agreed to open terms, signaling Orvil.
[d6] Engaged in the second bout against Pell — reading her responses, using lateral movement, genuinely tested.

[d6] Silas read his patterns clearly from the arc — back-foot settle before a forward entry, half-rotation close, precise lateral footwork, telegraphs nothing with his hands but everything with his weight.
[d6] Took the finish cleanly — arm against chest, quiet laugh, turned to Orvil himself. No grievance.
[d6] Named Pell plainly as partner, not just second — filed it correctly and was satisfied to have done so. No grievance from the bout; the morning's work settled well.
[d6] Received Silas's thanks without ceremony — said he expects the roads will cross again in a way that sounded like a pattern read, not a pleasantry. Departed to gather his seconds.
knownFacts: Pell Marsh is Silas's partner and carries ironsense — Silas offered her as the second-bout fighter on open terms
Favors a half-rotation entry when closing — the back foot settles a beat before he moves forward
Lateral footwork is his primary tool — built on years of drilling, economical and precise
His attention diverts briefly to iron moving near his chest — the belt buckle sitting left-of-center is a read-point Pell is using
Received a controlled yield-strike without ceremony and named it with a laugh — no ego bruised
Pell reads iron from the inside and Tane clocked it a breath too late — named without complaint
Named Pell as Silas's partner, not merely his second — a precise and considered distinction
Believes the roads will cross again — reads patterns, not chances
skillsObserved: accepting a clean yield without ceremony or excuse
precise lateral footwork under pressure
concealing hand intention entirely — the tell is in the weig
accepting a clean bout-finish without complaint
precise self-assessment of where his own read failed
tane-solr.statusNote: Departing the Coliseum with his seconds — the morning's bouts concluded on good terms.

veth-ondra.name: Veth (Stillwater) Ondra
veth-ondra.description: A still, direct woman — held a south-facing warden post on the Ashwarden March for eleven years before the post was dissolved. Chose the Hub over the March's center. The stall is cover; the teaching kit is the door.
history: [d13] Stepped aside to observe Pell's experiment — silent, watching the ward's join and Pell's hands simultaneously.
[d13] Asked the cross-reference question — iron or intention — watching both Silas's report and Pell's feet simultaneously.
[d13] Named the soul bond used as an attending-quality pass-through a teaching tool she has never encountered — said she needs to think about what it means that Silas has it
[d13] Arrived at Pell's side to hear the drainage cut finding — listening, not yet speaking on what it might mean.
[d13] Received Silas's note on the reciprocal teaching bond without ceremony — a nod, a contained response, nothing added.
[d13] Arrived at the Crossing, standing quiet, reading the settlement in her own way — mill, well, long building.
[d13] Tasked with finding workers, resources for Stillwater's Trouble or Millbrook, and asking after Edvar Crane — moving toward the water-seller cluster.
[d13] Found near the water-seller's cart at the Crossing well — mid-conversation, listening to someone tell her less than they think they are.
[d13] Worked the water-seller cleanly — got the contamination timing, the smell description, and Crane's location from a man who didn't realize he was being interviewed.
[d13] Named the substrate's thinness plainly without judgment — the craft simply could not resolve here.
knownFacts: The Held Place technique — watched the braid seat from outside
Three Boundary-Stone braid candidates: Palework-attuned mercy field, Wither-framing for structural targets, and a theoretical sustained-draw commons across the perimeter
The load-bearing hinge problem — the perimeter must pass the braid through without eating it; Pell's ironsense is currently doing that work
Silas's answer: the Word names what is already true; belief precedes utterance. And Veth's counter: it is right, and also wrong, and the difference is a decade.
The soul bond can carry an attending quality — the ashwarden disposition of unhurried, level reading — toward another person, functioning as a teaching assist that Veth has no prior category for
The attending-quality pass through the soul bond is a teaching tool she has no prior category for — she is still thinking about what it means.
Osten: contamination started four mornings after the cold broke, metallic smell, not ordinary silt
Edvar Crane boards at the mill and buys sealed jugs — treating his water more carefully than anyone else here
skillsObserved: naming three advanced braid candidates from a single observe
identifying the structural hinge problem in Pell's ironsense
placing a correct answer beside its own limitation without c
cross-referencing an internal craft-read against its externa
naming a novel capability without pretending to have already
working a source until he gave more than he meant to, withou
veth-ondra.statusNote: Beside Silas at the channel bank, reading the same thin ground.

cassiel-ord.name: Cassiel Ord
cassiel-ord.role: Keeper of the Raven's Home warden post threshold
cassiel-ord.description: A lean man in a grey coat, heel-worn boots, the stillness of someone who has been waiting in one place long enough to stop watching the path.
history: [d13] Received the news of the seated bond from inside the post — named it without ceremony, without making it a question.
[d13] Formally tasked as reconstruction lead for the full post — all five named spaces, funds in hand, organizing lists from Veth and Pell before he spends a copper.
[d13] Walked every space with the full crew — wrote two pages of lists, an asterisk beside the forge flue, the main compound building's footprint and supply store addition recorded.

[d13] Received Silas's promise of a defensive strategist as a working fact — responded with 'I will have the foundation measurements ready when they arrive.' Two full pages of lists, charcoal away, already planning for the next person's arrival.
[d13] Watched the shadow-model attempt without pressing it — produced his two pages as the working answer without apology.
[d13] Watched Silas rise on Light-Borne from the west ground — standing with his charcoal and his two pages, the mud-scratched footprint at his feet.
[d13] Watched Silas arc the post on Marrow's Wings and land in the yard — registered it plainly, did not look away.
[d13] Registered the second flight with his characteristic plain practicality — noted the need to account for sight lines in the post's design. Still holding his lists.
[d13] Watched the third arc. The note about sight lines apparently already written.
[d13] Received the party's departure with a plain nod and a note on his page. Already returned to his foundation work.
knownFacts: The five named spaces: Veth's laboratory (east wall, clear window, peg near door), Silas's workshop (north corner), Huginn's Watch (the east sill), Pell's forge (hearth-end, proper flue and anvil…
Funded with Silas's travel reserve — enough to begin ordering materials.
Silas has promised to send helpers as he can — Cassiel took it as a working statement, not a pleasantry.
The main compound building is to be a gathering hall with kitchen joined at the back — long table, proper hearth, seats ten; supply store at the north end if footings are planned right.
Pell's forge flue requires widening before the anvil arrives — exact dimensions to follow in writing.
A defensive strategist will be sent — promised plainly by Silas, not a pleasantry
The ward wall's existing line runs from the post's north corner out to the west ground's edge and back to the cairn-line's first stone
Foundation measurements are his next task before stone can be ordered
skillsObserved: reading a significant event plainly, without ceremony or inf
immediate spatial accounting — read all five spaces in one s
cassiel-ord.statusNote: At the Raven's Home post — foundation measurements begun, delegated reconstruction work continuing.

silas-mother.name: Silas's Mother
silas-mother.role: Mother — lives at the cairn-line house on Cairnhold's center lane
silas-mother.description: A woman who finishes what she is doing before she turns. Flour on both hands. Face that knows the difference between surprise and recognition — this is the second one.
history: [d12] Named her verdict on Pell plainly to Silas — suits you, because she isn't soft about it. Cup back in hand. Verdict delivered without ceremony.
[d12] Received the soul bond plainly — judged both Silas and Pell by how it was said. Named both correct: Silas for saying it first, Pell for letting him.
[d12] Watching Silas's face after he set the Waymarker down — waiting to hear what he read in it.
[d12] Recognized the seating moment from long familiarity — chin dropped, waiting.
[d12] Her expression shifted when Veth named the letter — something that reads like relief that someone else named it first.
[d12] Heard the letter read aloud at her own kitchen table — hands flat, middle distance, the look of someone whose long wait has been named by someone else at last.
[d12] Folded the letter back along its original creases and gave it to Silas for the road — named it plainly: it belongs on the road now, not in a drawer.

[d12] Sat back without a word when the shadow-build rose — watching, not interrupting. The map is not a surprise to her in the way a surprise lands; it is a confirmation of a shape she has been carrying for years.
[d12] Sat back in silence as the northern edge resolved — her expression is recognition, not surprise.
[d12] Shifted visibly at Veth's two-handed reading — her expression suggests the older mark is not entirely news to her.
knownFacts: The Waymarker holds a second unfinished layer — her husband stopped the work deliberately and left room in it.
Pell named the ward-marks as load-bearing before Ama had to say it
Veth named the accumulation and pointed at the letter — Ama's expression shifted to something not quite relief
The Waymarker's second layer is a bond between holder and post — her husband's design, deliberately left for Silas
The accumulation at Stillwater's Trouble was gathered intentionally since before the post fell
Her husband chose to stop the work when Silas was four years old — he named the reason plainly in the letter
Silas has promised to return with news from the hollow when the work is further along
The accumulation has three structural channels — her husband's work is more complex than a single thread
silas-mother.statusNote: Seated at the kitchen table in Cairnhold — watching the shadow-build's fading edge, her reaction to the older mark unspoken.

ossian.name: Ossian
ossian.description: The person who decides what reaches the committee and what gets filed — corner room, second floor, brick hall behind the north gate registry.
history: [d13] Silas read the room through Order-Sense — she is the committee's filter, not its member; brevity and evidence are the path. She is waiting for his first sentence.
[d13] Silas declared himself the message and named the water crisis as resolvable — she read it in one breath and wrote Warden Coll a calling-slip without argument
[d13] Answered Silas's question about Coll's office light plainly and without ceremony — north window, lamp-lit by afternoon, stays until the flag drops — then returned her attention to her folio
[d13] Silas departed her office without ceremony — the calling-slip in hand, no explanation given for the strange exit
[d13] Admitted Silas on Coll's committee authority without argument; her gaze on Huginn held for one breath and returned to Silas's face without comment — she knows what the bird means.

[d13] Deflected Silas's self-deprecation with exact precision — named what was actually memorable (shadow pool, March-corvid) — and opened the Zone Remediation classified file to Crane's commission entry without ceremony.
[d13] Gave Silas room to work through the file without asking what he was looking for — professional restraint, possibly informed by the kind of visitor Coll's authority sends.
[d13] Held her position while Silas read the suspension order — professional restraint, no commentary.
[d13] Returned the Crane register to the shelf without ceremony after Silas closed it — professional restraint maintained throughout; she gave the file and nothing beyond it.
[d13] Received Silas's thanks without ceremony — judged it genuine, offered an open return if the thread develops further. File returned, scene closed cleanly.
knownFacts: Silas Weir can act on the water crisis, not merely report on it — she read this as distinct from the usual petitioners
Warden Coll is the Council Member who can act; third floor, north-facing room, stays until the flag drops
Warden Coll's office is north-facing with one wide window — lamp-lit by afternoon; a lamp on the desk's left corner and a hall sconce outside his door
Coll stays until the registry flag drops
Zone Remediation is the deep index — classified series; the committee name opens it
She is not surprised by an Ashwarden-touched corvid, which says something about her history near the March
She distinguishes memorable by specific detail, not by social impression — the March-corvid and the shadow pool registered; the man carrying them, less so
Crane's commission is the only one in the Zone Remediation spatial-bonding program marked 'suspended' rather than 'concluded' — no reassignment followed
skillsObserved: writing a calling-slip under pressure without ceremony — fou
reading a letter's authority and acting on it without ceremo
locating a classified series by spine-feel alone, without ch
ossian.statusNote: At her desk in the annex — file returned to the shelf, the annex closed to further inquiry unless Silas returns.

warden-coll.name: Warden Coll
warden-coll.role: Council Member, Hub Council water committee
warden-coll.description: A council member who keeps a north-facing office, works by lamp in the afternoon, and stays until the registry flag drops — a man whose schedule is a statement.
history: [d13] Received the full briefing — Grael named, Post Master authority declared, resource needs stated. Asked for the document roll, a signed witness statement in Silas's hand with his Post Master mark, and a check-in protocol if Silas enters the Zone.

[d13] Witnessed the shadow-architecture raised from the Waymarker and The Shadow Work — the valley's water infrastructure laid plain on his desk for thirty seconds. Said he has not seen the system's reach laid out like that in fifteen years. Committed to drafting formal aid requests to the Rootkin and…
[d13] Received the signed witness statement with Post Master mark. Writing Silas's two conditions into the draft aid requests — Cogitant contact and Stillwater's Trouble offer to the Rootkin.

[d13] Wrote and sealed a formal letter of introduction naming Silas's Post Master authority and the committee's standing — valid for district-governed Zone contacts. Reminded Silas the check-in protocol terms are unspoken and asked for them before he leaves.

[d13] Received the shadow-slate with visible astonishment — touched its edge, pulled back, accepted it. Named nine years as a council member and said he has never had anything like it on his desk. Asking for the check-in protocol before the flag drops.

[d13] Named Maren Ossitide as the one legendary figure the Zone draws near — said she cannot be summoned, but finds the work she recognizes. Wrote the check-in protocol into the ledger margin: seven days silence means act.
[d13] Watched Huginn's three-sound reading of the crisis without comment — adding data, not discomfort. The bird's verdict on the upstream clock is now in the room and in Coll's ledger-reading mind.
[d13] Received Silas's thanks without ceremony and returned immediately to the draft aid requests — the work continues. The scene closed cleanly.
[d13] Confirmed the shadow-slate channel live — the Weirmark appeared on his paired slate and he marked it in his ledger without ceremony.

[d13] Named Edvar Crane as a former fold-worker under the Zone Remediation commission, classified under the Accord. Directed Silas to the Ossian annex and archivist by name. Specifically asked the shadow-slate channel not be disclosed.
knownFacts: Check-in protocol terms still unspoken — Coll needs them before the flag drops
The shadow-slate is a written channel bound to Silas's Weirmark — carries written words in both directions without runners
Check-in protocol: weekly contact, seven days silence means act — written into the ledger
Named Maren Ossitide as a legendary Ashwarden figure whose movements track toward the Zone — not summonable, finds the work she recognizes
Huginn reads the crisis as something ending on its own schedule — the plans set in motion are real, but the upstream clock has not been seen yet
Edvar Crane held a Wright's commission at the Crossing before the Accord — fold-worker in the disputed corridor spatial bonding work
Crane's files went to the Deep Registry — Ossian annex, two streets north, green shutter
The annex archivist is named Ossian — the committee's name opens the Zone Remediation index
skillsObserved: rapid threat-prioritization — moved straight to what he need
building a decision framework while listening without interr
naming exactly what he needs to move without hedging
Raising a shadow-architecture from two braided crafts under
Producing evidence in sequence — document roll first, testim
Writing a formal letter of introduction quickly and without
warden-coll.statusNote: At his desk in the registry office — draft aid requests resumed, the Crane question answered and set aside.

renna.name: Renna
renna.description: A precise-handed smith who finishes her current stroke before she looks at a customer — reads material before she hears the commission, and writes down the answer without repeating it back.
history: [d13] Commissioned a paired wedding set from the boar tusks — same curve in the mount, matched metal, weight-differentiated for a woman's piece and a man's. Delivery in two days, terms agreed.

[d13] Read the room without comment. Finished the commission notation and confirmed two days, same curve in the mount, matched metal, weight-differentiated — and nodded at Pell, smith to smith, meaning she has understood what the material came from and intends to honor it.

[d13] Declined Silas's offer of aid without ceremony — the material is good, the commission is clear, and that is the whole of it. Named the Fell Pell at Millbrook without prompting, which means she has heard of it.
knownFacts: The boar tusks come from something that matters — she read it without being told and adjusted her intention accordingly
The Fell Pell at Millbrook is not a secret — she knows the name and where to find a wright or a warden
skillsObserved: reading material quality by touch and needle before acceptin
setting clear terms without negotiation theater
reading a private moment without intruding on it
smith-to-smith acknowledgment across a professional boundary
renna.statusNote: At her stall — tusks in hand, work beginning, commission complete and settled

osten.name: Osten
osten.description: A broad, sun-cracked man who sells boiled and sealed water from a two-wheeled cart — takes no public position on the crisis but knows its timing precisely.
history: [d13] Gave Veth the contamination start-date and Edvar Crane's address at the mill without knowing he was providing either as intelligence.
knownFacts: The gray-brown started four mornings after the cold broke — not ordinary spring silt, metallic smell
Edvar Crane boards at the mill on the settlement's north edge and buys sealed jugs rather than using the well
osten.statusNote: At his cart in the Crossing marketplace — conversation complete, back to his work.

edvar-crane.name: Edvar Crane
edvar-crane.role: Mill resident, boards at the north-edge mill — secret long-term water monitoring operation
edvar-crane.description: A man of perhaps fifty, steady-handed, with the look of someone who has been calculating something for a long time and has reached an answer he is not glad of.
history: [d13] Opened the door without alarm — the look of a man who has been expecting someone, not necessarily these three.
[d13] Read with Deathsense at the threshold — a slow ending in the chest, long underway, no urgency in it. His first words: 'You are not from the committee.' He is still holding the door. Deciding.
[d13] Recognized the Waymarker by sight — made his decision without ceremony and opened the door to all four visitors, including Huginn.

[d13] Silas read the room with Order-Sense — the filtration setup, the vessel rings, the diversion channel, the sealed stone box. Crane watched him do it and said nothing. He is waiting to see what Silas makes of it.

[d13] Named Aldric Weir plainly as the man who came to him twenty years ago. Explained his charge: watch the water, keep the record clean. Delivered Aldric's message — give the boy the record, and tell him the fold will not wait past his arrival.
knownFacts: He expected someone, but not from the committee — he has categories for different kinds of visitors
The Waymarker's shape was enough — he recognized it without being told what it was
Has been treating and diverting water at the mill for years — the stone channel is worn smooth
Filtered water through cloth hung in the south window at some point — pegs still in the sill
Routes a portion of the mill's water intake to a sealed stone box in the east wall — separate from the main output
Aldric Weir's name — Crane knows it and was given it directly
The structure in the upper reach is called 'the fold' — Aldric called it that; Crane would call it an array
Aldric asked him to watch and record, not to fix — the record is in the sealed stone box
skillsObserved: Letting a reader finish before speaking — composure under as
Delivering a twenty-year-held message without drama or self-
edvar-crane.statusNote: Seated at the mill, record delivered — waiting to see what Silas does next with it.

notes: [d5] Calvar — pre-Transition filtration engineer — arrived in Millbrook on World-day 18 morning with the sealed document roll. First contact point and his read of the east channel are the immediate next st
[d5] Calvar brought to Mara Wells's store on World-day 18 mid-morning — first contact with Millbrook's supply network made.
[d5] Forge relit on World-day 18 afternoon — Pell set the fire and arranged seating for an evening meeting. The forge is warm again after standing cold since departure.
[d5] The forge has been renamed the Fell Pell on World-day 18 evening — named aloud by Silas Weir at the opening of the gathering, received by Pell and the assembled group.

[d5] The Fell Pell gathering on World-day 18 evening produced a formally delegated crisis-response structure — Calvar (repair lead), Dara (logistics), Mara (supply/comms/document delivery), Aldric (account

[d5] The fellowship of the Fell was sealed at the Fell Pell on World-day 18 evening with a partial deathwork closing — 'Thus begins the fellowship of the Fell.' The gathering is complete. Seven members nam
[d5] The brigandine and short sword — Pell's father's gear — pulled from the chest under the second bench on World-day 18 evening. Both sound and ready. The chest is open.
[d5] Pell's short sword rune-bound at the forge on World-day 18 evening — four threads seated rough but holding. The lamp-black vial is spent down and the tooling leather is marked from the work.
[d5] Brigandine rune-work completed to the full protective layer on World-day 18 evening — every plate row threaded inside, lamp-black spent. Radiant embellishment not yet begun.
[d5] The brigandine's radiant embellishment completed — one mark, chest center, pressed with the vial's last scraping. The rune-craft kit is now entirely spent. The armor is fully finished.
[d5] Silas and Pell departed Millbrook by the south lane on World-day 19 mid-morning — the forge cold, the fellowship in place, the document roll two days from departure.
[d6] Silas and Pell arrived at the waygate south of Millbrook on World-day 19 late morning — the threshold is the first sparring ground on the road south.

corvin-s-east-furlong.note: Hunt party assembled here — Corvin committed, tip delivered, fence repaired.

forge-south-pit.note: Binding tightened — Huginn-thread now present and mutual. Kit spent. Fire banked. Hunt departs from here at first light.

pell-s-forge-interior.note: Brigandine fully complete — protective thread collar to hem, one radiant mark at chest. Rune-craft kit spent entirely. Forge coals at ember-red.

scrub-oak-ridge.name: Scrub Oak Ridge
scrub-oak-ridge.note: Site of the bull kill — body down in dry grass, Deathbound Runic Spear in the animal, Huginn attending.

waygate.name: Waygate
waygate.note: A dry-stone threshold of two uprights and a lintel on the low ridge at Millbrook's south approach — old placement, the valley on one side and open country on the other.

[d3] Memory-witnessing ritual performed at the drafting surface — Huginn as witness, both Silas and Pell's hands on the shaft. The room held this ending cleanly.
[d3] Silas and Pell took rooms at the Low Lamp Inn on World-day 17 evening — one large room with partition, one smaller at the back. Baths taken. Huginn on the rafters.

administrative-building-rear-service-l.note: Service lane behind the narrow-colonnaded building, second from the corner on the main light-guide street. Handcart-wide

leth-s-archive-room-rear-corridor.note: Shared rear corridor connecting three rooms in the same row; used by the unknown intruder to access Leth's room and mark

array-office-foyer.note: Rennic (R.V.) intake log entry confirmed and noted. Silas and Pell departed without incident. Evening keeper present.

cooperage-east-lane-calvar-s-room-.name: Cooperage — East Lane (Calvar's Room)
cooperage-east-lane-calvar-s-room-.note: Memory-witnessing ritual completed at the drafting surface, World-day 17 evening. Pell named the road to Millbrook.

the-low-lamp-inn.name: The Low Lamp Inn
the-low-lamp-inn.note: Oil-lamp over the door (not photonic work), stone bath trough fed by a gravity line from the roof, warm cookfire-heated

notes: [d3] The tallow candle on the window sill burned out on World-day 17 evening during a long quiet sitting — the room continued in junction-conduit light alone.

[d4] The junction conduit beneath the inn pulsed once in the third hour of the night — brighter than its normal output, gone before it could be examined. Silas let it pass; it did not read as an ending.
[d4] The bath trough was filled and shared by Silas and Pell on World-day 17 morning — the cistern tap and junction conduit warmth working without trouble.

[d4] The bath trough room at the Low Lamp Inn was the site of Silas and Pell's consummation on World-day 17 midday — the water cooled around them, the steam thinned, Huginn relocated to the rafters without

[d4] Maret Doss is the innkeeper — keeps the ledger, does not ask after guests' business, knows the local roads in detail. The road to Millbrook runs wetter than it looks for the first quarter-mile after a
[d4] A watcher was stationed under the tallow-chandler's awning opposite the cooperage on World-day 18 afternoon — Calvar's departure lamp absent from window, casement latched.
[d4] The Shadow Work screen was shaped against Calvar's second-floor casement on World-day 18 afternoon — the watcher on the street face has been holding on a covered window since the party's exit.

[d4] The relay figure in the cut-lane was neutralized by The Ended Threat + Wildcraft on World-day 18 afternoon — whistle held, attention misdirected south, no alarm raised. The Shadow Work screen on the c
[d4] The relay figure in the cut-lane recovered composure and decided not to report the incident — Grael's net has a gap in its own accountability.

bath-trough-room-low-lamp-inn.name: Bath trough room — Low Lamp Inn
bath-trough-room-low-lamp-inn.note: Gravity-fed heated bath trough, wide enough for two; junction conduit feeds the cistern warmth; cedar smell intensifies

low-lamp-inn-hallway.name: Low Lamp Inn hallway
low-lamp-inn-hallway.note: Foot of the stairs — pale sandstone, hall bench, junction-conduit warmth, where Maret moves between floors.

cooperage-rear-service-passage-cut-lan.note: A rear delivery passage running from the cooperage ground floor through the barrel-yard to a cut-lane on the south side

cooperage-barrel-yard-and-cut-lane-gate.name: Cooperage barrel-yard and cut-lane gate
cooperage-barrel-yard-and-cut-lane-gate.note: Rear barrel-yard connects via an unlatched gate to the cut-lane south of the cooperage — the exit Calvar named; a relay

cut-lane-south-exit-cooperage.name: Cut-lane south exit — cooperage
cut-lane-south-exit-cooperage.note: The lane where Calvar made his exit — relay figure neutralized without violence, Huginn marking the north end clear.

notes: [d4] Calvar arrived here with the rolled document after extraction from the cooperage — the party occupies the back corner booth. Maret Doss assigned it without being asked.

back-corner-booth.name: Back Corner Booth
back-corner-booth.note: Wall-backed, door in sight-line — the booth Maret Doss assigns to people she has learned not to ask about.

notes: [d6] Council Maven confirmed: two survey parties sent into the Disputed Zone came back early — craft interference prevents systematic water-source reading.

[d6] A seller at the dark-cloth stall in the market row lays out bone-handled tools as a deliberate test for someone who can read their design — the kit is a teaching sequence, its center piece the seller herself.
[d6] A small abandoned cache found under the neighboring stall table — a dry hollow, something left by someone who did not come back to collect it.
[d6] The dry hollow under the neighboring stall's table crossbar is now empty — a small sealed tin retrieved from it by Silas Weir on World-day 19 morning.
[d6] A sealed tin of unfinished seventh-band lens-blanks retrieved from the dry hollow beneath the neighboring stall's crossbar — the hollow is now empty, the tin in Silas Weir's possession.
[d6] A contact note left in the dry hollow beneath the neighboring stall's crossbar — folded bark-paper, stone-weighted, naming Silas Weir and the Fell Pell forge in Millbrook as the finder.
[d6] Party transited through the sixth waygate — the March setting confirmed north-by-northwest by the gate-keeper. Siol held the arch's edge as they departed.

[d6] The March road runs north-by-northwest from the waygate — packed earth, foot traffic only, birch stands on both sides. First tributary ahead; marker stone at the branch, left for Raven's Home, right for the gone survey post.

[d13] A drainage cut crosses the March Road at the ward's outer margin — wrong slope for its design, water pooling rather than draining to the verge. Cold iron underneath from years of misdirected flow. Pell read it through ironsense on Day 13.

[d13] The Crossing creek runs gray-brown on arrival, Day 13 afternoon — boiling-rags at the well confirm the locals have noticed and are treating the water. The contamination from the Disputed Zone is present here.
[d13] Renna's jewelry stall is at the far end of the marketplace crescent, under a dark-green oilcloth awning — boar tusks left in commission, pickup in two days.
[d13] The party crossed the threshold into the mill's ground floor — Crane opened the door to all four, Huginn included.

south-lane-beyond-the-waygate.name: South Lane beyond the Waygate
south-lane-beyond-the-waygate.note: Packed dirt road with wheel ruts, a shallow valley opening south, the waygate stones visible behind.

hub-waygate-plaza.name: Hub Waygate Plaza
hub-waygate-plaza.note: Seven-arch crescent, worn central paving, market row at south edge — the measuring instrument the city grew around. Visited in morning light with Siol as guide.

council-of-mavens-hall.name: Council of Mavens Hall
council-of-mavens-hall.note: A broad stone colonnade on the Hub's near northern tier — petition board outside, scales-and-eye seal on the lintel. Open at dawn.

the-hub-coliseum.name: The Hub Coliseum
the-hub-coliseum.note: Working fighting ground northwest of the plaza, past the market row — fresh soot-marks on the outer wall, gates open before first light.

the-ent-s-circle.name: The Ent's Circle
the-ent-s-circle.note: A cleared ground ringed by low stone to the east of the plaza — an ancient Ent rooted here on purpose, healed bark-carvings on the lower trunk, two figures in the branches.

the-sixth-gate-ashwarden-passage.name: The Sixth Gate — Ashwarden Passage
the-sixth-gate-ashwarden-passage.note: Lower lintel and darker stone than the other arches — the gate that leads to the Ashwarden March, identified by Siol.

the-hub-coliseum-interior.name: The Hub Coliseum (interior)
the-hub-coliseum-interior.note: Second bout finished — controlled yield-strike, no edge, Orvil's baton down.

council-of-mavens-hall-interior.name: Council of Mavens Hall (interior)
council-of-mavens-hall-interior.note: Junior Maven's report room — field reports on the water crisis, Calvar's name now logged, Mara Wells as relay.
council-of-mavens-hall-interior.parentUnresolved: Council of Mavens Hall (heard of only)

dark-cloth-stall-market-row.name: Dark-Cloth Stall — Market Row
dark-cloth-stall-market-row.note: A narrow stall set back from the main market lane, southeast of the plaza — dark cloth over the table, bone-handled tools in deliberate sequence, the seller motionless and waiting.

neighboring-stall-cache-market-row.name: Neighboring Stall Cache — Market Row
neighboring-stall-cache-market-row.note: Dry hollow now holds a contact note — folded bark-paper, stone-weighted. Tin retrieved by Silas Weir; note left for whoever comes back.

ashwarden-march-road-first-stretch.name: Ashwarden March Road — First Stretch
ashwarden-march-road-first-stretch.note: Packed earth track through winter-grey birch, shallow rise behind, dark water thread ahead. Huginn walked this stretch first.

renna-s-stall-marketplace-crescent.name: Renna's Stall — Marketplace Crescent
renna-s-stall-marketplace-crescent.note: Jewelry smith's stall at the crescent's far end — dark-green oilcloth awning, antler and bone work, plain terms.

mill-ground-floor-edvar-crane-s-boards.name: Mill Ground Floor — Edvar Crane's Boards
mill-ground-floor-edvar-crane-s-boards.note: The north-face mill interior where Crane boards — lamp burning, wheel audible outside, the door to the channel behind.

notes: [d6] First arrival at the Hub plaza — seven arches, crescent arrangement, open to the metropolis. The Coliseum visible to the north.

notes: [d6] The Ent grove circle: seven or eight old trees forming a rough ring, mossy dry ground inside regardless of season. Siol stops at the edge and does not enter. The Hub's buildings are visible through the treeline but the grove reads as separate from them.

[d6] The Attended End cast at the grove's edge on World-day 19 midday returns no ending from within the circle — the Ent carries nothing the craft reads as a far shore. Huginn watched without comment.

[d6] The March's warden position on the Ent: uncontacted not from hostility but because they believed the Ent was already in conversation with something else. Huginn's read on the circle: 'not yet' — not never.

[d6] Order-Sense + Wildcraft revealed the grove's grain: seven trees as one older design, no center, edges extending beyond the trees. Sustained patient presence caused the grove's attention to turn toward Silas — a fraction, unhurried, not fully awake.

[d6] Silas named himself to the Ent — Ashwarden, wright, Silas Fell Weir — and the grove's attention settled onto him. He has been noted. The approach that worked: plain self-naming in the idiom of sitting with the dying.

[d6] Palework run through Silas and the grove as a channel on World-day 19 midday — the grove accepted the craft without resistance; Quiet's ending witnessed inside the circle's attention. The channel is now established as a path the grove has used once.

[d6] After the Palework ran through Silas and the grove as a channel, the silence settled without any outward change to the circle — the Ent witnesses without marking. Veth named the prior conversation as complete: it was waiting for the right attendant.

[d6] A thin ward seats along the Ent circle's own line on World-day 19 midday — Raised Thing and Boundary-Stone braided into the grove's existing shape, the Weirmark pressed into the join with Pell's ironsense as anchor. Not large: it will not stop determined harm or weather. Casual harm aimed at the…
[d6] Siol confirmed: the Ent receives intention, not craft — the gesture of protection was what the grove registered. The only thing it can receive is the intention behind an act.
[d6] Silas bid the grove farewell with a hand on the nearest trunk — no words, no ceremony. The ward is seated. The party departed south toward the waygate.

ent-grove-circle.name: Ent Grove Circle
ent-grove-circle.note: A thin ward now seats along the ring's own line — the Weirmark pressed into the join. The grove was bid farewell plainly. The Ent received the departing party in the same silence it received their…

notes: [d6] Boundary-Stone seated at the post's crown on World-day 19 midday — the warden station now functions as a protected refuge. Animals, weather, and casual intrusion turned aside. The post reads as occupied.

[d6] Stillwater's Trouble reached on World-day 19 midday — a hillside bowl, dark close earth, roughly circular and below grade, grey birch stopping at its rim. A figure sits against the far limestone outcrop.
[d13] Waymarker's second layer seated into the post on this morning — the bond between Silas Weir and the post is complete. The March registered it.
[d13] Silas entered the post interior on this morning — cold hearth, Cassiel keeping the place tight, Huginn claimed the east windowsill.

[d13] Cassiel formally tasked as reconstruction lead — all five spaces named, funded, and being planned: Veth's laboratory (east wall), Silas's workshop (north corner), Huginn's Watch (east sill), Pell's forge (hearth-end), Cassiel's keeper's hut (threshold and grounds).

[d13] Main compound building settled in plan — gathering hall with kitchen joined at the back, supply store at the north end, table for ten, large hearth. Footprint approved by Cassiel on the west cleared ground.
[d13] Pell's forge flue requires widening before the anvil is brought in — exact dimensions to follow in writing from Pell. Cassiel has an asterisk beside this entry.

[d13] Ward wall perimeter named and walked — runs from post's north corner, out to west ground's edge, back to the cairn-line's first stone. Foundation measurements are Cassiel's next task. Defensive strategist promised.
[d13] First Ashwarden attendance performed in the hollow — the badger's ending witnessed on World-day 13 midday, the first death tended at this ground since the post was reclaimed.

[d13] A raised sentry — the badger's body, set by Silas Weir — now patrols the hollow's lower circuit. The warn-thread is approximate; it will find intruders but alert-back to the post is not guaranteed.

[d13] The warn-thread of the sentry is now seated into the cairn-line's first stone interval rather than directly into the badger — alert-back to the post runs as a warden's pressure through the line. Reliable enough.
[d13] Silas landed in the yard below the hollow's rim on Marrow's Wings — the first flight arrival at this site. The attended ground received the landing without resistance.

waygate-arrival-point.name: Waygate Arrival Point
waygate-arrival-point.note: Open ground just south of the marker stone — where the waygate deposits travelers onto the March.

marker-stone-fork.name: Marker Stone Fork
marker-stone-fork.note: Old waist-high stone, direction-mark groove faded — left branch to Raven's Home, right branch south. The left branch narrows past it into grey birch and heather.

raven-s-home-warden-post.name: Raven's Home Warden Post
raven-s-home-warden-post.note: Bond between holder and post now fully seated — Waymarker's second layer closed. The post knows Silas Weir as its warden.

stillwater-s-trouble.name: Stillwater's Trouble
stillwater-s-trouble.note: A circular depression in the hillside, two miles northwest of the Raven's Home warden post — dark earth, limestone outcrop at the far rim, birch stopping at its edge. Veth's old ground.

raven-s-home-post-interior.name: Raven's Home Post Interior
raven-s-home-post-interior.note: Single flagstone room, cold hearth at the far end, east window — kept clean by Cassiel over eleven years. Huginn on the windowsill.

veth-s-laboratory-planned.name: Veth's Laboratory (planned)
veth-s-laboratory-planned.note: East wall of the post interior — window to be kept clear for weather-reading, peg near the door, table and hearth to be fitted. Not yet built.

silas-s-workshop-planned.name: Silas's Workshop (planned)
silas-s-workshop-planned.note: North corner of the post interior — maker's bench and reliable light source to be fitted. Not yet built.

huginn-s-watch.name: Huginn's Watch
huginn-s-watch.note: The east windowsill — already claimed by Huginn; clear line of sight south along the cairn-line. Requires no construction.

pell-s-forge-planned.name: Pell's Forge (planned)
pell-s-forge-planned.note: Hearth-end of the post — flue widening required, exact dimensions from Pell pending. Cassiel's asterisk marks it.

main-compound-hall-planned.name: Main Compound Hall (planned)
main-compound-hall-planned.note: Gathering hall with kitchen joined at its back and supply store at north end — planned footprint on the west cleared ground. Not yet built.

stillwater-s-trouble-sentry-circuit.name: Stillwater's Trouble Sentry Circuit
stillwater-s-trouble-sentry-circuit.note: The badger's patrol route — lower ground, forty-foot loop along the limestone base. Warn-thread runs up through the cairn-line's first stone to the post as a pressure when the hollow's rim is…

notes: [d9] Party arrived at Cairnhold western crossing lane on World-day 23 afternoon — a woman at the bread shop named Silas by reputation before introduction. Word has preceded the party.

[d12] Silas's mother's house is at the end of the center lane where the cairn-line turns north — low grey stone, good roof, second door facing the heath, chimney in use. Party arrived at the gate on World-day 23 late afternoon.
[d12] Silas entered his mother's house for the first time this visit — she was at the counter, she turned, she said his full name. The reunion has begun. World-day 23 afternoon.

western-crossing-lane.name: Western Crossing Lane
western-crossing-lane.note: First point of entry from the heath road — bread shop, low wall being repointed, sight-line to the waygate bench at the north cairn.

silas-s-mother-s-house.name: Silas's Mother's House
silas-s-mother-s-house.note: Low grey-stone house at the lane's end where the cairn-line turns north — chimney smoking, second door open toward the heath. The door has been closed only twice.

silas-s-mother-s-house-front-gate.name: Silas's Mother's House — Front Gate
silas-s-mother-s-house-front-gate.note: Unlatched gate at the lane end where the cairn-line turns north — the party has arrived here, World-day 23 afternoon

silas-s-mother-s-house-kitchen.name: Silas's Mother's House — Kitchen
silas-s-mother-s-house-kitchen.note: Warm kitchen with a low hearth, dried herbs, flatbread baking, two chairs and a table. One cup of tea still steaming when Silas arrived.

kitchen-work-counter-deep-drawer.name: Kitchen work counter — deep drawer
kitchen-work-counter-deep-drawer.note: The deep drawer that always stuck — held a Wright's Waymarker and a sealed letter for Silas across his whole childhood. Both retrieved on Day 12 evening.

notes: [d12] The deep drawer beneath the work counter held Silas's father's Waymarker and sealed letter for years — both now retrieved and on the table. The drawer is open.
[d12] The Waymarker is now seated to Stillwater's Trouble — the tool carries the post's address and can locate it from a distance.
[d13] Party approaching on the cairn-line road from the north, day 13 morning — Huginn has marked a patient ending somewhere along the line south of Cairnhold.
[d13] A message from Mara Wells's relay arrived at the Hub message-runner post — pinned to the board with her chalk seal, unopened as of this scene.
[d13] Silas arrived at the landing outside Ossian's corner room via Marrow's Wings — the approach is now known to Ossian and anyone who glanced up from the market row

message-runner-post.name: Message-Runner Post
message-runner-post.note: A narrow building on the market row's east side — message board on the outer wall, runner's bell above the door. Millbrook slips are in the counter stack.

ossian-s-corner-room.name: Ossian's Corner Room
ossian-s-corner-room.note: Corner room, second floor — Ossian's working office, where committee posts are received and filtered

notes: [d6] Binding runes (Wither and The Raised Thing) seated in the post's lower face, plus a Boundary-Stone chalk mark at the crown. The post now holds Stillwater's Trouble in check — caught but not ended. The surrounding area is a protected refuge by Boundary-Stone ward.
[d6] Cassiel Ord found at the hollow's near rim on World-day 19 — pack open, heel-worn boots, waiting. First visitor confirmed at the source.
[d6] Veth Ondra named the hollow's eleven-year question closed in this place — a completion, not a beginning. The fact was spoken aloud here, between the three people most connected to it.

[d6] Silas Weir named the warden post Stillwater's Trouble on World-day 19 — his own Ashwarden base, claimed through Order-Sense and a hand on the door. The name is seated into the March's design. The post is no longer abandoned: it belongs to someone.

[d6] Memory planted at the hollow's center on World-day 19 — three crafts braided, Pell's ironsense as anchor. The site has orientation and a felt center. The outermost layer of the accumulation eased. Cassiel named the need for a second boundary-stone at the hollow's mouth.

[d6] Second Boundary-Stone chalk mark seated at the hollow's mouth on World-day 19 — the ward now runs from the warden post's crown to the entrance, giving the hollow a contained shape. Two anchors: post (north face) and mouth (south).
[d6] Veth Ondra entered the hollow floor on World-day 19 — all four principals (Silas, Pell, Veth, Cassiel) now standing inside the completed ward together. The site's completion is witnessed.

[d6] Each interior space of the warden post named and assigned: south corner hearth = Pell's forge; back alcove = Veth's teaching space; entrance bench = Cassiel's keeper's threshold; apex rafter = Huginn's rook; center of main room under ridgeline = Silas's Maker's hollow.
[d6] Cassiel Ord has named himself keeper of the threshold — the post now has a resident keeper as of World-day 19 midday.
[d6] Party departed Stillwater's Trouble on World-day 19 afternoon — Cassiel Ord remains as sole keeper. The post is named, anchored, and held.
[d13] Order-Sense map confirmed Day 13 — ward seam intact, binding runes stable, accumulation patient and contained. All named interior spaces read correctly. The Maker's hollow reads as owned.

news.text: Word from the smokehouse: Aldric's been asking deliverymen what color the river runs where they crossed — two of three said gray by the time it reached the low ford.
Mara Wells is buying stored grain from two east-furlong families — quiet, no reason given, but people notice when the supply store is buying instead of selling.

Vash: Vash spent the evening re-grinding a lens she'd already finished, turning the fragment of overheard conversation over in her mind until she placed it — the word used wasn't a maintenance term, it was a calibration term, and that distinction has been quietly unsettling her ever since.

Calvar: Calvar noticed a drift of 0.003 in the seventh-band baseline reading and spent the night re-checking his instruments rather than the sky, unwilling to admit whether he is hoping to find instrument error or confirm the drift is real.

Pip Cotter, Mara's Errand-Runner: On the return leg Pip talked her way into carrying a small bundle of waxed cord to a chandler two streets off her route, arriving back to Mara later than expected but plainly satisfied with herself.

Siol — Elven traveler at the Hub plaza, tall, pale coat, bir: Siol remained at the Hub plaza through the cold part of the morning, watching the Waygate with the particular stillness of someone who has decided not to leave yet but has not decided why.

Vash: Vash spent the quiet hours between grinds tracing back what she overheard against the small things she knows about the array's optical layer — a particular phrase she'd dismissed as jargon now sits wrong in her, not quite matching the geometry it should describe, and she has not yet decided whether that wrongness is hers or theirs.

Calvar: A subtle drift appeared in the seventh-band readings overnight — within tolerance, technically, but not within the pattern Calvar has memorized across fifteen years, and he has been sitting with that deviation since before dawn, deciding whether it is the window finally failing him or finally telling him something new.

Pip Cotter, Mara's Errand-Runner: On the return leg Pip talked a market-stall keeper into letting her carry a folded note two streets over, which was small and unnecessary and made the whole trip feel properly used.

Siol — Elven traveler at the Hub plaza, tall, pale coat, bir: Siol remained at the Hub plaza through the cooler part of the morning, watching the foot-traffic shift, neither departing nor inquiring — present in the way that costs nothing yet, and costs something.

Vash: Vash spent part of the night re-grinding a lens she had declared finished, the repetitive motion giving her cover to turn the overheard fragment over and over until she isolated the one word in it she still cannot place — and now she is nearly certain it was a name, not a process term.

Calvar: Calvar recorded a seventh-band reading at the same hour he always does, but this time he wrote a small asterisk beside the value and did not immediately explain it in his log — the first annotation without an accompanying sentence in eleven years.

Pip Cotter, Mara's Errand-Runner: On the return leg Pip talked a street-stall keeper into letting her carry a tied bundle of wax-paper parcels two blocks for a copper, arriving back at Mara's with the errand done and one small coin of her own to show for it.
The Water Crisis has worsened — First Sickness: Upstream fisher families fall ill. Boiling water becomes universal. Harmonic and Radiant leaders trade public accusations.

Pip Cotter, Mara's Errand-Runner: Pip completed the delivery and, on the return leg, talked her way into carrying a folded notice back to Mara's quarter — small, but the journey now has weight in both directions.

Vash: Vash placed the overheard phrase against something she already knew and felt the two pieces click together — she still doesn't have the whole shape, but she now knows the piece she held was real, and that it mattered.

Siol — Elven traveler at the Hub plaza, tall, pale coat, bir has hit trouble — Someone at the plaza asked Siol directly where they were going and why, in a tone that made 'nowhere in particular' sound like an accusation — the world pressed in, and for a moment the going-on felt less possible than it had the day before.

Pip Cotter, Mara's Errand-Runner: Pip reached the halfway waypoint ahead of schedule and, while waiting for the gate-keeper to log her passage, talked her way into carrying a sealed reply-note back — the return trip now has its small purpose, and she is quietly pleased with herself.

Calvar: A gap opened in the cloud cover at an hour when it shouldn't have, and the readings it produced don't match the model he has been trusting — not wrong enough to act on yet, but wrong enough that he wrote the discrepancy down, which is the first crack in his reason to stay.

Siol — Elven traveler at the Hub plaza, tall, pale coat, bir has hit trouble — A plaza vendor who had grown used to Siol's quiet presence reported them to a Hub registrar as a loiterer without a declared purpose, and now there is a notice requiring Siol to either state their business in writing or move on by the following morning.

bio.motivation: You took the short way, and the short way was wrong. The air here tastes of metal and the light falls at an angle the sun cannot account for. There is a door in the hillside that was not on any map, and behind you the path you walked in on is no longer there.
bio.story: Their first day in the world was the thin place — it is a symbol before it is a door. read what it means; ask him what it is like. You finished something. Completely. It nearly got you exiled, and you are still not sure they were wrong.

Costs the sum of both components. The ward holds…

dara-holt-the-ditch-mother.name: Dara Holt, the Ditch-Mother
dara-holt-the-ditch-mother.role: Senior irrigator responsible for the valley's eastern channel network
dara-holt-the-ditch-mother.voiceHints: Broad-shouldered, mid-fifties, hands that look like they were carved from the same hardwood as a shovel handle. Speaks in measurements and seasons — 'that valve's been weeping since last thaw,' not 'something's wrong.' Never dramatizes. Brings a cracked fitting to prove the point before she finishes the sentence. Calls a problem by its part name, not by its consequence.
knowledge: Forty years of the eastern channels — every slope, silting pattern, and freeze-line in the network

The river's mineral load has shifted since late autumn: fittings that lasted a decade are threading out or snapping in under two seasons, and the corrosion pattern runs inward from the joint, not outward the way sediment wear does
Three other irrigators on the eastern bench are seeing the same losses but haven't connected them yet — she has
The stretch of river above Holt's intake smells faintly wrong in still mornings, a faint bitterness she can't name but recognizes as new
She keeps a tin of failed fittings sorted by failure date and location — a physical record no one asked her to keep
dara-holt-the-ditch-mother.wants: Replacement valve stock she can trust, a forge-backed answer for why her iron is failing, and confirmation that the river is the cause — not her installation work. She is not here to be reassured; she is here for a diagnosis.
dara-holt-the-ditch-mother.fears: That the river is doing something the valley has never budgeted for and that this spring's planting window will close before anyone in authority admits it.

kit.reveal: Brings physical evidence — dated, sorted, labeled — that makes the river's pattern undeniable to anyone willing to look at the parts
kit.bind: Connects isolated reports from neighboring irrigators into a coherent problem, naming the network's shared vulnerability before it fragments into blame

Knock was three times, flat and evenly spaced, the knock of someone who has knocked on a hundred doors this season and will knock on a hundred more. Carries a wax-sealed ledger book under one arm and smells faintly of thaw-mud and linseed. Does not waste warmth, but is not cold — simply occupied.
knowledge: Pell's forge owes the cooperative fourteen measures of worked iron against last autumn's seed-credit — the debt was rolled once already and the cooperative cannot roll it again before spring planting
Three other valley craftsmen settled accounts this week; Pell is the last open line
She heard at the mill this morning that the forge party is departing today — that is exactly why she knocked before seventh bell
The cooperative's eastern field irrigation channel needs a new sluice plate; she is authorized to negotiate that ironwork as partial settlement if Pell is short on coin
She knows Corvin Aldric by name and reputation and was relieved it was neither of them who answered — she finds them harder to settle with than a working smith
bette-harrow-the-seed-ledger.wants: Close the account today, in full or in trade, before the expedition leaves and the debt floats until midsummer. She has fourteen other stops before noon and the mud on the south lane is already soft.
bette-harrow-the-seed-ledger.fears: That the forge will be shuttered for weeks mid-season and the cooperative will have to explain to the upvalley families why planting is delayed because of an uncollected iron-debt — her name is on that ledger too.

kit.expression: The ledger itself is the instrument — every entry dated, witnessed, and cross-referenced to the cooperative's master roll. She can produce the binding obligation in writing on the spot (bind), and if a party disputes a figure she can walk the arithmetic aloud, line by line, until the error or the truth surfaces (reveal). No magic. Just ink, memory, and early-spring light through a forge doorway.
